name: KNIME publication and workflow reproducibility audit script chain
purpose: Formal workflow index for the article and workflow audit process, from OpenAlex article collection through article-audit table generation.

handoff_terms.automatic: The next step consumes files written by this step without required manual editing.
handoff_terms.semi_automatic: The script produces or consumes an intermediate state that may require manual action, such as institutional article download, human article assessment, workflow retrieval, or manual KNIME opening/execution checks.
handoff_terms.llm_automatic: The script runs automatically after deterministic preprocessing, but uses an LLM for bounded judgment tasks that previously required human review.
handoff_terms.manual: The step is not currently represented by a deterministic script and must be performed or edited by a human before the next scripted step is meaningful.

article_audit_chain.name: Collect OpenAlex KNIME article records
article_audit_chain.notes: Raw OpenAlex records are the source for bibliometric summaries and rank-based article download attempts.

article_audit_chain.name: Build OpenAlex bibliometric summaries and most-cited seed table
article_audit_chain.notes: The current Makefile default writes the top 100 citation-ranked seed table, matching the current structured assessment scope.

article_audit_chain.name: Attempt article PDF downloads by citation-rank range
manual_action_required: Inspect failed, non-PDF, or paywalled download attempts.
Download articles manually where institutional access is available and lawful.
Place manually obtained PDFs under data/original/articles/.
Preserve download-attempt manifests under data/processed/audit/logs/.
article_audit_chain.notes: This step is semi-automatic because OpenAlex links do not always provide retrievable PDFs and some articles require university subscription access.

article_audit_chain.name: Extract semantic article HTML with GROBID
input: local GROBID service: docker run --rm --init --ulimit core=0 -p 8070:8070 grobid/grobid:0.9.0-crf
article_audit_chain.notes: The only article parser in the current pipeline. Uses GROBID processFulltextDocument to create TEI XML, then renders semantic HTML with title, abstract, body sections, paragraphs, and bibliography. The full local run processed 83 PDFs with 0 failures. Use article-grobid-html-all to regenerate all outputs.

article_audit_chain.name: Generate structured article assessment candidates
manual_action_required: Review data/processed/audit/article_deterministic_assessments.json for over-detected regex matches, undefined fields, and mismatches with the curated audit.
Do not overwrite data/processed/audit/old_article_assessments.json from this step; use the generated file as a deterministic comparison and review artifact.
article_audit_chain.notes: This step creates a deterministic candidate assessment without network access and without reading the curated assessment JSON. The script reads the OpenAlex seed table, the canonical local article registry, processed article HTML with source-PDF metadata, audit questions, and the workflow-reference inventory. The registry is the source of truth for local article bibliographic metadata and PDF/HTML identity; OpenAlex is kept as the citation-ranked seed and provenance source. DOI is treated as an indivisible primary key, so matching uses only the full normalized DOI-derived registry key or an explicit no-DOI registry key, never DOI suffixes, tails, or fragments. The script writes a standalone JSON file with article_audit_fields, linked_resources, source pdf_file values, and undefined values where deterministic rules cannot decide. Use DETERMINISTIC_LIMIT and RANK to test subsets.

article_audit_chain.name: Classify linked resource URL types with LLM
manual_action_required: Run a small pilot first, for example LIMIT=1 or RANK=<rank>, before a full paid run.
Review data/processed/audit/article_llm_url_assessments.json for URL type assignments that are unsupported by article text.
Do not open or fetch URLs for this step; the LLM must classify URL types only from article text evidence.
article_audit_chain.notes: This step reads linked_resources from the deterministic assessment and asks the LLM only to replace linked_resources[*].type values that are undefined. It sends article metadata, URL lists, and paragraph-local article context from the processed article representation. It does not call the URLs under audit and does not change article_audit_fields.

article_audit_chain.name: Review and correct article audit fields with LLM
manual_action_required: Run a small pilot first, for example LIMIT=1 or RANK=<rank>, before a full paid run.
Review data/processed/audit/article_llm_flag_assessments.json for corrected fields that are unsupported, weakly supported, or inconsistent with deterministic and URL-type evidence.
Do not overwrite data/processed/audit/old_article_assessments.json from this step; use the generated file as an LLM-filled comparison and review artifact.
article_audit_chain.notes: This step reads the URL-typed candidate assessment and asks the LLM to review and correct only article_audit_fields.description_audit_fields and article_audit_fields.flag_audit_fields. The script sends article metadata, current article_audit_fields, typed linked_resources, audit questions, prompt rules, and selected line-referenced excerpts. It ignores flag_audit_support and preserves all fields outside article_audit_fields. The output remains a standalone candidate file for review.

article_audit_chain.name: Refresh quote-backed article-audit support
manual_action_required: Run with LLM_MODE=validate-support to ask the LLM whether candidate quotes directly support each audit flag.
Review data/processed/audit/logs/llm_support_validation_decisions.jsonl, especially records where llm_supported differs from deterministic_validator_result.
Use LLM_APPLY_REJECTIONS=--llm-apply-rejections only after reviewing disagreements and accepting that LLM false decisions should remove candidate quote support.
Use RESET_FLAGS_FROM_DESCRIPTIONS=--reset-flags-from-descriptions and APPLY_FLAG_REMOVALS=--apply-flag-removals only for an intentional classification rebuild, not for routine support refresh.
article_audit_chain.notes: This is a provenance/support updater, not a replacement for the article assessment itself. With LLM_MODE=off it uses only deterministic regex and validation rules. With LLM_MODE=validate-support it still finds candidate quote windows deterministically, then asks the LLM a bounded yes/no evidence question for each candidate quote. By default, LLM decisions are logged for audit rather than applied as destructive edits; the existing audited flags are preserved. The LLM must decide whether the quote directly answers the audit question for that flag; vague keyword matches should be rejected. Current processed article input is GROBID HTML.

article_audit_chain.name: Create or update workflow-reference inventory
manual_action_required: Retrieve workflow archives, workflow directories, repository snapshots, or supplements where possible.
Record failed or not-attempted retrievals.
Open retrieved workflows in the local current KNIME environment.
Record import/opening outcome, execution outcome, screenshots, missing extensions, missing data, and other failure notes.
article_audit_chain.notes: Human semi-automatic work: first inspect data/processed/audit/knime_downloadable_workflow_references.json. For each record, use workflow_references, supplement_references_checked, evidence_extracted_text_lines, and processed_text_file to understand where the article reported a workflow or supplement. If more context is needed, open the matching record in data/processed/audit/old_article_assessments.json and inspect linked_resources.workflow_urls, linked_resources.supplement_urls_or_dois, linked_resources.notes, workflow_artifact_status, and evidence_notes. Then follow the recorded URLs manually in a browser or with a download tool: publisher supplements, repositories such as GitHub or Zenodo, KNIME Hub pages, older workflow portals such as myExperiment, and project websites. Save obtained workflow archives, workflow directories, HTTP traces, and screenshots under data/original/workflows/<article-or-doi-safe-directory>/. Update download_result with the retrieval status, downloaded_files, workflow_files_found, failed_references, directory, and notes. Open retrieved workflows in the current local KNIME environment when feasible, then update manual_knime_opening_tests with opened_in_knime, execution_result, artifact, screenshot_directory, and notes. If nothing is obtained, record the reason explicitly rather than deleting the reference.

article_audit_chain.name: Build article-audit summary table source
article_audit_chain.notes: Use --fail-on-mismatch when table values should match the manuscript exactly.

article_audit_chain.name: Build KNIME-use workflow-reporting table source
article_audit_chain.notes: Uses the workflow inventory summary count for articles with successfully downloaded workflows.

out_of_scope_for_current_scripts: Final VWRS weighting function f(A, I, E, M, D, X, C, R)
Fully automatic article PDF retrieval through institutional subscription access
Fully automatic workflow artifact retrieval from every article-specific repository or portal
Fully automatic KNIME workflow execution and repair
